title: QRF — outer lane beacon
summary: A distress squawk lit up on the outer approach vector. Someone needs to verify whether it is a real casualty or a spoofed lure.

objectives.text: Head to the dock and check the approach traffic board for the beacon vector.

objectives.prompt: The beacon coordinates look real, but the signature is weak. You can sortie immediately, log it for the duty officer, or flag it as a probable spoofed lure.

choices.label: Sortie immediately on the last known vector.
choices.outcome: You burn hard toward the signal. Whatever is out there, you will find out first.

choices.label: Log it formally with the duty officer and wait for tasking.
choices.outcome: The report goes up the chain. By the time a response is authorized the window may have closed.

choices.label: Flag it as a probable spoofed lure and advise against response.
choices.outcome: Your assessment is logged. If you are wrong, someone else pays the price.

title: CAP scramble — find the contact
summary: A task unit just sortied fighters for an unresponsive heavy past the exclusion perimeter. Someone in station intelligence wants eyes on the intercept before it is sanitized.

objectives.text: Check the promenade traffic displays — CAP scramble routes are posted in plain sight if you know what to look for.

objectives.prompt: You have a partial picture of the intercept vector. You can sell it to independent traders, pass it to station authority, or hold it for later leverage.

choices.label: Sell the vector to independent traders who need routing clarity.
choices.outcome: Credits now. The information dissolves into market noise quickly.

choices.label: Pass the picture to station authority through a formal channel.
choices.outcome: The duty watch thanks you. Your name goes into a short list of reliable sources.

choices.label: Hold the intelligence for future leverage.
choices.outcome: You know something no one else does. That is a commodity with a short shelf life.

title: Salvage tug rescue
summary: A licensed salvage crew is pinned down by a hostile intercept in the outer belt. No escort on station; the window is narrow.

objectives.text: Go to the dock to pick up the tug's last telemetry packet from the dispatch terminal.

objectives.prompt: The tug is broadcasting emergency codes. You can burn to their position as escort, coordinate a handoff to a fleet picket, or report the situation and collect a finder fee.

choices.label: Burn to their position and cover their withdrawal.
choices.outcome: You go in hot. The crew makes it out; your hull takes some attention getting there.

choices.label: Coordinate a handoff to the nearest fleet picket.
choices.outcome: The picket takes the job. Slower, but the crew survives and your record looks clean.

choices.label: Report the position and claim the finder fee — let someone else risk the approach.
choices.outcome: The guild pays the tip. The crew's odds depend on who answers next.

title: Ghost hull — IFF fraud investigation
summary: Security confirmed a spoofed transponder. The hull is not in any registry; it passed through approach control twice. Somebody wants to know who authorized the blind eye.

objectives.text: Visit the dock gate where the ghost hull was logged. Someone on shift will remember the approach.

objectives.prompt: A dock worker remembers the hull — and who waved it through. You can take the lead to security, sell it to the independent press, or use it as personal leverage over the contact.

choices.label: Hand the lead directly to station security.
choices.outcome: The investigation opens formally. The contact disappears within hours.

choices.label: Sell the story to the independent press.
choices.outcome: The scandal runs; security is embarrassed. You make good money and a few enemies.

choices.label: Confront the contact privately and demand a quiet favor.
choices.outcome: You have leverage — until the contact decides the risk of silence outweighs the cost of removing it.

title: Transit lane seven — convoy debrief
summary: Three freighters reported a weapons lock and evasive contact before the escort called weapons free. The cargo manifests have not been reconciled; somebody wants a neutral party to walk the docks.

objectives.text: Walk the promenade trade floor — the crews will be talking.

objectives.prompt: You piece together what happened. One cargo manifest does not match the dock scanner log. You can file a straight debrief, flag the discrepancy privately to the guild, or sit on it.

choices.label: File a complete and accurate debrief with all parties.
choices.outcome: The guild and security both have the picture. The discrepancy goes on record.

choices.label: Flag the manifest discrepancy to the guild privately and let them decide.
choices.outcome: The guild owes you discretion. The anomaly may or may not surface.

choices.label: Sit on the discrepancy — knowledge is a resource.
choices.outcome: Nobody knows you know. That only helps as long as you are the only one who found it.
